anthropic.note: Enforced via daily lockfile /tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt — at most one audit fire per UTC day. Each fire = 1 Opus extended-thinking call (~$2). No Haiku callers in active code; if reintroduced, pair with explicit enforcement and update this cap.

gemini.note: Hard-coded in gemma4_helper.py _GEMINI_DAILY_LIMIT (added 2026-04-25). Currently only audit_check + audit_fire_debate use it; cap has plenty of headroom.

ai_consensus.anthropic:
**Audit verdict: "LOW" is misleading — projected=0 means the scanner found 26 timers but couldn't map any to AI callers. That's the real risk.**

(a) **Runaway loops:** Anthropic cap relies on `/tmp/audit_check_ai_last_run.txt` — `/tmp` is ephemeral; a reboot/container restart wipes the lockfile and re-fires Opus (~$2/fire) every cycle until next reboot. No mutex, no atomic write shown.

(b) **Missing caps:** Gemini cap lives only in `gemma4_helper.py`; any new caller bypassing that helper is uncapped. No global budget ceiling, no per-hour cap, no kill-switch.

(c) **2× rate:** Gemini → 200/day, still under headroom (cheap). Opus → if lockfile fails, 2 fires = $4/day = ~$1,460/yr; loop scenario unbounded.

(d) **Most likely silent burner:** **audit_fire_debate** — debate loops can self-tri
ai_consensus.grok:
**Risk Audit:**

(a) **Runaway-loop**: Lockfile-based Anthropic cap is brittle in container/k8s environments. A crash-restart loop or shared /tmp race can bypass the "one per UTC day" guard, firing $2 Opus calls repeatedly.

(b) **Missing daily caps**: Only Gemini calls routed through `gemma4_helper.py` are capped. Any direct Gemini usage or new Haiku calls bypass all enforcement.

(c) **2x rate blow-up**: Anthropic at 2 calls/day = $4/day ($1,460/yr). Acceptable but erodes the "we barely use it" assumption.

(d) **Stealth burn king**: Anthropic Opus (`audit_fire_debate`). High cost, infrequent, lockfile-hidden — easiest to burn thousands before operator notices.

**Overall**: LOW today, but lockfile fragility + fragile scoping = high tail risk once trading bot is live. Add proper rate-lim
